headline: Social Media Age Verification Law Takes Effect for Under-16s
context: Parliament mandated social media platforms verify user age with accounts for those under 16 requiring parental consent.

cards.big: Social media age verification mandatory for under-16s from March 2026
cards.sub: Platforms must implement technical verification within 6 months of law

cards.big: Estimated 3.8 million Malaysian children under 16 have social media accounts
cards.sub: 78% created accounts using false birthdates to bypass existing age gates

cards.big: Platforms given 6 months to implement government-approved verification system
cards.sub: Options include MyKad-linked verification and parental consent apps

cards.big: Digital rights groups warn age verification creates surveillance infrastructure
cards.sub: Children privacy data from verification could be vulnerable to breaches

cards.big: Age verification tests whether child protection justifies creating new digital identity databases
Under-16 age verification on social media. Enforcement depends on technical design, not legislative intent alone.
